text: She was brought to the operating room.
After intubation and a normal bronchoscopy, she was positioned in the left lateral position.
A 2 cm port was placed in the posterior axillary line at the 8th intercostal space, through which a 10 mm thoroscope was passed.
On examination, there were significant adhesions between the right upper lobe and the chest wall along the major fissure, likely from her prior vats.
An additional port was placed at the anterior axillary line in the fourth intercostal space.
There was an endometrial implant on the parietal pleura that was removed and sent for pathology.
A ring forceps was used in conjunction with bovie electrocautery to take down the adhesions in order to prevent recurrence with a loculated pneumothorax.
Multiple blebs were observed on the lateral surface of the middle lobe and the apex of the upper lobe.
These were removed with wide resection using an endoscopic stapler.
The apical parietal pleura was scored with bovie electrocautery.
The pleurectomy was performed by bluntly dissecting the apical parietal pleura away from the chest wall.
Next, a mechanical pleurodesis was performed with a folded bovie scratch pad on a ring forceps.
Visualization of the right hemidiaphragm demonstrated a significant diaphragmatic eventration.
This eventration was repaired with placement of a 15 by 15 centimeter Vicryl mesh placed in order to cover all macroscopic and any microscopic occult defects in the diaphragm.
The mesh was then fixed in place with multiple figure of eight number one Vicryl stitches.
500 milligrams of doxycycline was injected into the pleural space as a chemical pleurodesis.
Finally, the surface of the mesh was coated with 10 grams of tisylfibrin sealant to cover any remnant microperforations.
Two 19 French Blake chest tubes were replaced.
